heading: Branch profits tax
label: 884 Branch profits tax
notes: References in TextThe FSC Repeal and Extraterritorial Income Exclusion Act of 2000, referred to in subsec. (d)(2)(B), is , , . For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title of 2000 Amendments note set out under and Tables.Pub. L. 106–519114 Stat. 2423section 1 of this titleNov. 15, 2000
Prior ProvisionsA prior section 884 was renumbered .section 885 of this title

(e)(3). , substituted “withholding tax” for “2nd tier withholding tax” in heading and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows:Pub. L. 100–647, § 1012(q)(2)(B)“(A) .—If a foreign corporation is not exempt for any taxable year from the tax imposed by subsection (a) by reason of a treaty, no tax shall be imposed by section 871(a), 881(a), 1441, or 1442 on any dividends paid by such corporation during the taxable year.In general“(B) .—No foreign corporation which is not a qualified resident of a foreign country shall be entitled to claim benefits under any income tax treaty between the United States and such foreign country with respect to dividends—Limitation on certain treaty benefits“(i) which are paid by such foreign corporation and with respect to which such foreign corporation is otherwise required to deduct and withhold tax under section 1441 or 1442, or“(ii) which are received by such foreign corporation and are described in section 861(a)(2)(B).”Subsec. children.heading: Imposition of tax
children.label: a Imposition of tax
children.text: In addition to the tax imposed by section 882 for any taxable year, there is hereby imposed on any foreign corporation a tax equal to 30 percent of the dividend equivalent amount for the taxable year.

children.heading: Reduction for increase in U.S. net equity
children.label: 1 Reduction for increase in U.S. net equity
A
children.text: the U.S. net equity of the foreign corporation as of the close of the taxable year, exceeds

children.label: B
children.text: the U.S. net equity of the foreign corporation as of the close of the preceding taxable year,

children.heading: Increase for decrease in net equity
children.label: 2 Increase for decrease in net equity
i
children.text: the U.S. net equity of the foreign corporation as of the close of the preceding taxable year, exceeds

children.label: ii
children.text: the U.S. net equity of the foreign corporation as of the close of the taxable year,

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The increase under subparagraph (A) for any taxable year shall not exceed the accumulated effectively connected earnings and profits as of the close of the preceding taxable year.

children.heading: Accumulated effectively connected earnings and profits
children.label: ii Accumulated effectively connected earnings and profits
I
children.text: the aggregate effectively connected earnings and profits for preceding taxable years beginning after , overDecember 31, 1986

children.label: II
children.text: the aggregate dividend equivalent amounts determined for such preceding taxable years.

children.label: A
children.text: U.S. assets, reduced (including below zero) by

children.label: B
children.text: U.S. liabilities.

children.heading: U.S. assets
children.label: A U.S. assets
children.text: The term “U.S. assets” means the money and aggregate adjusted bases of property of the foreign corporation treated as connected with the conduct of a trade or business in the United States under regulations prescribed by the Secretary. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the adjusted basis of any property shall be its adjusted basis for purposes of computing earnings and profits.

children.heading: U.S. liabilities
children.label: B U.S. liabilities
children.text: The term “U.S. liabilities” means the liabilities of the foreign corporation treated as connected with the conduct of a trade or business in the United States under regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Regulations to be consistent with allocation of deductions
children.label: C Regulations to be consistent with allocation of deductions
children.text: The regulations prescribed under subparagraphs (A) and (B) shall be consistent with the allocation of deductions under section 882(c)(1).

children.heading: Effectively connected earnings and profits
children.label: d Effectively connected earnings and profits

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “effectively connected earnings and profits” means earnings and profits (without diminution by reason of any distributions made during the taxable year) which are attributable to income which is effectively connected (or treated as effectively connected) with the conduct of a trade or business within the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: income not includible in gross income under paragraph (1) or (2) of section 883(a),

children.label: B
children.text: income treated as effectively connected with the conduct of a trade or business within the United States under section 921(d) or 926(b) (as in effect before their repeal by the FSC Repeal and Extraterritorial Income Exclusion Act of 2000),

children.label: C
children.text: gain on the disposition of a United States real property interest described in section 897(c)(1)(A)(ii),

children.label: D
children.text: income treated as effectively connected with the conduct of a trade or business within the United States under section 953(c)(3)(C), or

children.label: E
children.text: income treated as effectively connected with the conduct of a trade or business within the United States under section 882(e).

children.heading: Coordination with income tax treaties; etc.
children.label: e Coordination with income tax treaties; etc.
A
children.text: such treaty is an income tax treaty, and

children.label: B
children.text: such foreign corporation is a qualified resident of such foreign country.

children.label: i
children.text: on branch profits if so specified, or

children.label: ii
children.text: if not so specified, on dividends paid by a domestic corporation to a corporation resident in such country which wholly owns such domestic corporation, and

children.label: B
children.text: any other limitations under such treaty on the tax imposed by subsection (a) shall apply.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If a foreign corporation is subject to the tax imposed by subsection (a) for any taxable year (determined after the application of any treaty), no tax shall be imposed by section 871(a), 881(a), 1441, or 1442 on any dividends paid by such corporation out of its earnings and profits for such taxable year.

children.heading: Limitation on certain treaty benefits
children.label: B Limitation on certain treaty benefits
i
children.text: any dividend described in section 861(a)(2)(B) is received by a foreign corporation, and

children.label: ii
children.text: subparagraph (A) does not apply to such dividend,

children.label: i
children.text: 50 percent or more (by value) of the stock of such foreign corporation is owned (within the meaning of section 883(c)(4)) by individuals who are not residents of such foreign country and who are not United States citizens or resident aliens, or

children.label: ii
children.text: 50 percent or more of its income is used (directly or indirectly) to meet liabilities to persons who are not residents of such foreign country or citizens or residents of the United States.

children.heading: Special rule for publicly traded corporations
children.label: B Special rule for publicly traded corporations
i
children.text: the stock of such corporation is primarily and regularly traded on an established securities market in such foreign country, or

children.label: ii
children.text: such corporation is wholly owned (either directly or indirectly) by another foreign corporation which is organized in such foreign country and the stock of which is so traded.

children.heading: Corporations owned by publicly traded domestic corporations
children.label: C Corporations owned by publicly traded domestic corporations
i
children.text: such corporation is wholly owned (directly or indirectly) by a domestic corporation, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the stock of such domestic corporation is primarily and regularly traded on an established securities market in the United States.

children.heading: Secretarial authority
children.label: D Secretarial authority
children.text: The Secretary may, in his sole discretion, treat a foreign corporation as being a qualified resident of a foreign country if such corporation establishes to the satisfaction of the Secretary that such corporation meets such requirements as the Secretary may establish to ensure that individuals who are not residents of such foreign country do not use the treaty between such foreign country and the United States in a manner inconsistent with the purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Exception for international organizations
children.label: 5 Exception for international organizations
children.text: This section shall not apply to an international organization (as defined in section 7701(a)(18)).

children.heading: Treatment of interest allocable to effectively connected income
children.label: f Treatment of interest allocable to effectively connected income
A
children.text: any interest paid by such trade or business in the United States shall be treated as if it were paid by a domestic corporation, and

children.label: B
children.text: to the extent that the allocable interest exceeds the interest described in subparagraph (A), such foreign corporation shall be liable for tax under section 881(a) in the same manner as if such excess were interest paid to such foreign corporation by a wholly owned domestic corporation on the last day of such foreign corporation’s taxable year.

children.heading: Allocable interest
children.label: 2 Allocable interest
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “allocable interest” means any interest which is allocable to income which is effectively connected (or treated as effectively connected) with the conduct of a trade or business in the United States.

children.heading: Payor must be qualified resident
children.label: A Payor must be qualified resident
i
children.text: such treaty is an income tax treaty, and

children.label: ii
children.text: such foreign corporation is a qualified resident of such foreign country.

children.heading: Recipient must be qualified resident
children.label: B Recipient must be qualified resident
i
children.text: such treaty is an income tax treaty, and

children.label: ii
children.text: such foreign corporation is a qualified resident of such foreign country.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: g Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this section, including regulations providing for appropriate adjustments in the determination of the dividend equivalent amount in connection with the distribution to shareholders or transfer to a controlled corporation of the taxpayer’s U.S. assets and other adjustments in such determination as are necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this section.
